Shauna Schramke.short: Shauna Schramke paints quirky old houses as emotional portraits and portals.
Shauna Schramke.medium: Shauna Schramke transforms forgotten houses into timescapes and emotional portraits, celebrating homes with strong opinions and tender hearts.
Shauna Schramke.long: Shauna Schramke paints old, quirky houses as portraits of the introverted and overlooked. Her timescapes celebrate homes that refuse to let go—structures with painted wood, out of plumb foundations, strong opinions, and tender hearts.

Emily von Stuckrad-Smolinski.short: Emily von Stuckrad-Smolinski uses unconventional tools to create textured oil works.
Emily von Stuckrad-Smolinski.medium: Emily von Stuckrad-Smolinski crafts richly textured oil paintings using fingers, palette knives, and air compressors instead of traditional brushes.
Emily von Stuckrad-Smolinski.long: Emily von Stuckrad-Smolinski transforms oil painting through unconventional techniques, using fingers, palette knives, and air compressors. Her textured works emerge organically as materials guide each piece, creating a journey she compares to a runner's high.

Kurt Smolinski.short: Kurt transforms canvas through color-guided pouring, perfected by dedication.
Kurt Smolinski.medium: Kurt transforms canvas through intuitive color-guided pouring, a technique perfected through dedication. Between tennis and pickleball, he creates art.
Kurt Smolinski.long: Kurt transforms canvas through intuitive color-guided pouring, a technique he has perfected through relentless experimentation. This middle school artist balances his creative practice with tennis and pickleball, sharing only his finest work.

Linda Hart.short: Linda Hart transforms Dayton's decay into watercolor art celebrating hidden beauty.
Linda Hart.medium: Linda Hart captures decaying industrial buildings in luminous watercolors, revealing unexpected beauty in rust, texture, and nature reclaiming urban spaces.
Linda Hart.long: Linda Hart paints Dayton's crumbling warehouses and industrial landscapes in watercolor, finding beauty where others see decay. Her work reveals how time and nature transform forgotten structures into abstract compositions worth celebrating.

Ching Chung.short: Ching creates vibrant digital art infused with whimsy, color, and cultural joy.
Ching Chung.medium: Ching creates vibrant digital art blending cultural heritage with whimsy. Her work captures joyful moments in bright, imaginative colors.
Ching Chung.long: Ching transforms inspired moments into vibrant digital art that sparks joy and imagination. Drawing from her Asian heritage and design background, she captures whimsy through bold colors and cultural richness.

Dave Crowell.short: Artist and framer channeling observation and human experience into authentic work.
Dave Crowell.medium: Artist, designer, and founder of Custom Frame Services combines philosophy and history to create work that serves as a social engine for culture.
Dave Crowell.long: Self-taught artist and founder of Custom Frame Services since 1993, dedicated to authentic creative expression. Philosophy and history inform work that views art as a social engine, helping artists realize their vision through daily practice.

Bonnie Kuntz.short: Bonnie embraces uncertainty in watercolor, acrylics, and mixed media art.
Bonnie Kuntz.medium: Bonnie teaches watercolor and creates art celebrating life's unpredictability through animals, florals, and landscapes in watercolor and mixed media.
Bonnie Kuntz.long: Bonnie teaches watercolor while creating vibrant paintings of horses, animals, florals, and landscapes. Her work in watercolor, acrylics, and mixed media celebrates the adventurous uncertainty of art and life itself.

Marlene Steele.short: [Artist Name] transforms salvage into vibrant art through spontaneous creation.
Marlene Steele.medium: [Artist Name] upcycles building salvage into bold, vibrant paintings guided by intuition and the unpredictable beauty of each moment.
Marlene Steele.long: [Artist Name] transforms salvaged materials into daring, colorful art without plans. Each piece evolves spontaneously, reflecting emotion and celebrating the joy of unpredictable creation that makes every work unique.

Jo Anne Vincent.short: Jo Anne Vincent crafts sterling silver jewelry with vibrant beads and gems.
Jo Anne Vincent.medium: Jo Anne Vincent designs sterling silver and copper jewelry, combining pearls, hand-blown glass, agate, turquoise, and lapis in unique patterns.
Jo Anne Vincent.long: Jo Anne Vincent transforms sterling silver and copper into unique jewelry pieces. She combines pearls, Balinese beaded beads, California hand-blown glass, and vibrant gemstones, guided by 13 years of exploring color, pattern, and texture.

Ariel White.short: Ariel White transforms animals into whimsical art with bright colors and playful joy.
Ariel White.medium: Ariel White paints animals in bright, cartoon style, turning the ordinary into whimsical art that sparks playfulness and laughter at Dayton Front Street.
Ariel White.long: Ariel White blends her genetics background and love of nature into whimsical animal paintings. Her bright, cartoon-style work celebrates the cute, silly, and odd, bringing joy and laughter to viewers at her Dayton Front Street studio.

Michelle Brandt.short: Self-taught artist capturing nature's joy in vibrant acrylics and watercolors.
Michelle Brandt.medium: Self-taught artist painting vibrant acrylics and watercolors inspired by garden birds and flowers, bringing joy through impressionistic realism.
Michelle Brandt.long: Self-taught artist creating impressionistic paintings in vibrant acrylics and watercolors. Inspired by nature's flowers and birds, each piece radiates joy through bold, happy colors. Member of Dayton Society of Artists with awards and exhibitions.

Michael Surber.short: Michael Surber creates poetic, primal paintings alive with vibrant color.
Michael Surber.medium: Michael Surber's experimental paintings blend bold textures and vibrant hues, honoring tradition while pushing boundaries with primal energy.
Michael Surber.long: Ohio-based artist Michael Surber creates poetic, experimental paintings that honor tradition while pushing boundaries. His work captivates with primal energy, bold textures, and vibrant color that invite viewers to find their own stories.

Nancy Shuler.short: Nancy Shuler blends color, texture, and design to create stunning works of art.
Nancy Shuler.medium: Nancy Shuler combines color, texture, and design to create art that invites viewers to pause and appreciate life's splendor through paint.
Nancy Shuler.long: Nancy Shuler, a Troy, Ohio artist with a BFA from Wright State, creates stunning works combining color, texture, and design. Her art invites viewers to pause and observe where dreams intertwine with reality through paint's transformative power.

Kim Ceccarelli.short: Kim Ceccarelli paints vibrant oils and pastels inspired by light and shadow.
Kim Ceccarelli.medium: Kim Ceccarelli creates luminous oil and pastel works exploring light and shadow, with pieces displayed from the Smithsonian to downtown Dayton.
Kim Ceccarelli.long: Kim Ceccarelli transforms her experiences as a nurse and world traveler into vibrant oil and pastel paintings. Her plein air works celebrate light and shadow, earning awards across Ohio and exhibition from the Smithsonian to galleries nationwide.

Cindy Callahan.short: Cindy creates textured acrylic art exploring color, form, and depth.
Cindy Callahan.medium: Cindy is a prolific Dayton artist working in acrylics and watercolor, celebrating the dynamic relationship between color, form, and texture.
Cindy Callahan.long: Cindy is a self-taught Dayton artist creating vibrant acrylic and watercolor paintings. She uses texture mediums, unique tools, and recycled objects to build depth and emotion through her exploration of color and form.

Lori Daugherty.short: Lori Daugherty creates vibrant encaustic art with intricate wax layers.
Lori Daugherty.medium: Lori Daugherty crafts bold abstract encaustic paintings using wax, pigment, and heat. Her layered works invite viewers on an enchanted visual journey.
Lori Daugherty.long: Lori Daugherty transforms encaustic wax into vibrant abstract art using torches and heat. Based in Dayton, she builds intricate layers of color and texture that invite viewers to explore depth and mystery in each captivating piece.

Pat McClelland.short: C. Pat McClelland transforms base-2 relationships into dynamic visual complexity.
Pat McClelland.medium: C. Pat McClelland explores dualities—warm/cool, order/chaos—merging them into complex visual compositions where loss and discovery yield completion.
Pat McClelland.long: C. Pat McClelland's paintings emerge from base-2 relationships: warm/cool, order/chaos, shape/line. These elements merge, overlap, and compete, creating dynamic compositions where sacrifice of early work yields new discoveries and ultimate completion.

Barbara Mandell.short: Barbara Mandell creates vibrant impressionist and abstract art for 47+ years.
Barbara Mandell.medium: Barbara Mandell creates colorful impressionist and abstract designs by hand, supporting MS and breast cancer research through her art sales.
Barbara Mandell.long: Barbara Mandell has created vibrant impressionist and abstract art for 47+ years, blending architectural lines and geometric shapes. Her colorful work enhances homes and businesses while supporting multiple sclerosis and breast cancer research.

Lindsey Tribelhorn.short: Self-taught artist creating visual portals to alternate realities.
Lindsey Tribelhorn.medium: Self-taught artist working in acrylics, murals, and mixed media to challenge perception and evoke states of visual transcendence.
Lindsey Tribelhorn.long: Dayton-based artist specializing in acrylic painting, murals, and mixed media. Work explores sensory perception and creates visual portals to alternate realities, inviting viewers on journeys of transcendence without drugs.

Roxanne Grooms.short: Artist weaves circles into rich narratives with earth tones and textured threads.
Roxanne Grooms.medium: An artist exploring the duality of circles through rich earthly hues, nurturing stitches, and twisted threads that invite imaginative play.
Roxanne Grooms.long: This artist transforms circles into dynamic characters within richly textured compositions. Earth tones, deliberate stitches, and twisted threads anchor each piece, inviting viewers to engage their imagination and discover personal narratives.

Larry Hensel.short: Larry Hensel transforms architecture and event photography into striking designs.
Larry Hensel.medium: Larry Hensel blends 30+ years of graphic design expertise with photography, creating striking architectural panels and vintage poster art.
Larry Hensel.long: Larry Hensel combines three decades of graphic design mastery with contemporary photography, capturing historical architecture and events. His work transforms photographs into compelling architectural panels and vintage-inspired posters.

Brian Mathus.short: Brian paints with emotional clarity, removing distortion to reveal truth.
Brian Mathus.medium: Brian paints figurative works with emotional clarity, layering color and mark-making to create ecstatic, vibrant compositions rooted in truth.
Brian Mathus.long: Brian creates vibrant figurative paintings that pursue emotional clarity through layers of color and mark-making. Drawing from Fauvism, Cubism, and African art, he removes distortion to reveal truth, building sublime compositions until ecstatic.

Mikee Huber.short: Mikee Huber transforms data into vibrant abstract art using pipettes and scrapers.
Mikee Huber.medium: Mikee Huber creates vibrant abstracts inspired by urban life, music, and science, using unconventional tools to explore human connection through color and line.
Mikee Huber.long: Mikee Huber blends intuition with visual data in her abstract paintings, inspired by circuit boards, topography, and 90s hip hop. Using pipettes and scrapers, she creates vibrant color fields exploring human connection and community through intersecting lines.

Pam Geisel.short: Pam Geisel creates bold graphic art quilts with striking patterns and messages.
Pam Geisel.medium: Pam Geisel fuses graphic design with quilting to create powerful abstract and landscape pieces. Her work explores typography and socio-political themes.
Pam Geisel.long: Pam Geisel brings graphic design sensibility to her art quilts, creating striking abstract patterns and representational landscapes. Based in Yellow Springs, Ohio, she incorporates words and typography to explore socio-political themes through textile art.

Erin Smith Glenn.short: Erin Morgan Smith Glenn creates art rooted in advocacy and HAIRitage.
Erin Smith Glenn.medium: Erin Morgan Smith Glenn explores advocacy, activism, and HAIRitage through her paintings, drawings, and mixed media works.
Erin Smith Glenn.long: Erin Morgan Smith Glenn channels advocacy, activism, and HAIRitage into her art. An educator and entrepreneur, she strives to leave the world better through creative exploration and continual growth.

Bruce Soifer.short: Award-winning photographer capturing fleeting moments of artistic creation.
Bruce Soifer.medium: Award-winning photographer with the eye of a painter, capturing dancers, musicians, artists, and nature's ephemeral moments of beauty and passion.
Bruce Soifer.long: Award-winning photographer with the eye of a painter and heart of an elephant, capturing fleeting moments of creation—from dancers leaping across stages to musicians making music, artists at work, and nature's magic in sunrises and storms.

Alisa H. Workman.short: Alisa H. Workman transforms darkness into vibrant floral paintings.
Alisa H. Workman.medium: Alisa H. Workman channels life's struggles into bold, abstract floral paintings bursting with vibrant color and energy.
Alisa H. Workman.long: Alisa H. Workman transforms personal tragedy into vibrant abstract florals. A preschool art teacher and geologist, she creates bold paintings with rich color splashes. Her work is collected across the US, Canada, and Europe.

Beth Borchers.short: Beth Borchers creates soul-stirring abstract art with bold colors and magic.
Beth Borchers.medium: Beth Borchers is a self-taught mixed media artist creating soul-stirring abstracts with intense colors, metallic accents, and layers of depth and meaning.
Beth Borchers.long: Beth Borchers creates captivating abstract mixed media art that stirs emotion and connects viewers to their hearts. Her bold use of color, metallic accents, and playful swirls reflect her love of nature, magic, and the beauty she sees in the world.

Amy Kollar Anderson.short: Amy Kollar Anderson layers glitter, mica, and paint to create surreal narratives.
Amy Kollar Anderson.medium: Amy Kollar Anderson builds densely layered paintings with glitter, mica, and foils, creating surreal narratives about nature, dreams, and transformation.
Amy Kollar Anderson.long: Amy Kollar Anderson creates surreal narrative paintings by layering glitter, mica, washi, and holographic foils with finger-painted puddles. Her work explores anxiety, humor, and transformation through zodiac-inspired series and environmental themes.

Erica Keener.short: Erica captures storms and skies in pastels, sharing nature's sanctuary.
Erica Keener.medium: Erica paints clouds and storms in bold pastels, transforming ordinary scenes into sources of comfort. Her award-winning work hangs throughout Ohio.
Erica Keener.long: Erica transforms fleeting skies and storms into pastel sanctuaries that bring solace to viewers. Working from her Preble County studio, she captures nature's beauty in award-winning pieces found in hospitals, galleries, and collections across Ohio.

Gretchen Durst Jacobs.short: Gretchen Durst Jacobs paints body-scale abstractions exploring nature and power.
Gretchen Durst Jacobs.medium: Gretchen Durst Jacobs creates body-scale abstract paintings merging intuitive gestures with geometric grids to explore nature, feminism, and climate change.
Gretchen Durst Jacobs.long: Gretchen Durst Jacobs paints large-scale abstractions that merge expressive gestures with geometric grids. Her work reclaims abstract painting through a feminine lens, exploring climate change and our sacred relationship with nature.

Lydia Williamson.short: Lydia Williamson paints whimsical 'NOODLE People' exploring human awkwardness.
Lydia Williamson.medium: Lydia Williamson creates playful acrylic paintings of long-limbed 'NOODLE People' that capture the humor and curiosity of human experience.
Lydia Williamson.long: Lydia Williamson's acrylic paintings feature her signature 'NOODLE People'—exaggerated, long-limbed figures exploring human awkwardness with humor. Her spontaneous style invites viewers to find their own meaning in each playful piece.

Christy Veres.short: Christy Veres creates bohemian folk art using reclaimed materials and woodburning.
Christy Veres.medium: Christy Veres blends bohemian folk style with reclaimed materials, creating unique woodburned art inspired by nature and fellow artists.
Christy Veres.long: Christy Veres transforms reclaimed wood, metal, and canvas into bohemian folk art through woodburning, carving, and painting. Her unique perspective celebrates nature while giving new life to forgotten materials at festivals and galleries.

Cydnie King.short: Cydnie L. King merges figures with botanicals in vivid, nature-inspired works.
Cydnie King.medium: Cydnie L. King creates vivid artworks where human figures meld into botanicals, exploring our inseparable bond with nature through inks and pastels.
Cydnie King.long: Cydnie L. King explores humanity's connection to nature through vivid works depicting figures merging with botanicals. Using inks, pastels, and pencils, she reveals how inseparable we are from the natural world around us.

Jeremy Nickoson.short: Jeremy captures nature's wonders through self-taught landscape photography.
Jeremy Nickoson.medium: Jeremy transforms landscapes from 56 National Parks into vibrant metal prints that inspire others to explore nature's wonders.
Jeremy Nickoson.long: Self-taught photographer Jeremy specializes in landscape and nature photography across 56 National Parks. His vibrant metal prints capture inspiring moments straight from camera, encouraging others to experience nature's beauty firsthand.

Tami Beale.short: Tami Beale layers vibrant oils into bold geometric abstracts from her Ohio studio.
Tami Beale.medium: Tami Beale crafts layered abstract oil paintings with vivid colors and textures. Her geometric compositions bring beauty and boldness to every space.
Tami Beale.long: Tami Beale creates vibrant abstract oil paintings with rich layers and textures from her Bellbrook, Ohio studio. Her geometric works blend bold colors with contemporary style. She believes art heals both creator and viewer alike.

Glenna Jennings.short: Glenna Jennings captures cross-cultural connection through intimate tablescapes.
Glenna Jennings.medium: Glenna Jennings uses photography to explore connection and food justice, documenting intimate moments where strangers become family across global tables.
Glenna Jennings.long: Glenna Jennings transforms personal memory into cross-cultural dialogue through her series At Table, photographing shared meals across continents. A food justice activist and educator, she merges art with social practice to reveal human connection.

Libby Rudolf.short: Expressive watercolorist capturing essence through color & form in pleinair work.
Libby Rudolf.medium: Expressive watercolorist weaving color & form to capture essence of scenes and people. Works from life, featured at Village Artisans Gallery.
Libby Rudolf.long: Expressive watercolorist weaving color & form to capture the essence of scenes and people through pleinair painting. Featured at Village Artisans co-op Gallery in Yellow Springs since 2013. Paints from life with passionate brushwork.

Art of Frozen Time (Jeff Smith).short: Jeff Smith captures golden hour sunsets with panoramic precision and light.
Art of Frozen Time (Jeff Smith).medium: Jeff Smith photographs fleeting golden hour moments, creating panoramic sunset images that evoke awe and bring peaceful solitude to healthcare spaces.
Art of Frozen Time (Jeff Smith).long: Jeff Smith chases the brief, stunning light of golden hour, capturing panoramic sunset vistas through bracketed exposures. His contre-jour technique creates signature sun stars that bring peaceful, evidence-based healing imagery to viewers.

Matt Blair.short: Matt Blair captures Dayton's architecture and culture through watercolors.
Matt Blair.medium: Matt Blair paints Dayton's historic landmarks and diverse architecture in playful watercolors. He works in both whimsical and realistic styles.
Matt Blair.long: Matt Blair celebrates Dayton through plein air watercolors and urban sketches. His paintings capture the city's architectural diversity and cultural landmarks in styles ranging from whimsical to realistic. His work was featured in a documentary.

Kathleen Caffrey.short: Anthrosercher explores history, culture, and courage through diverse media.
Kathleen Caffrey.medium: Anthrosercher weaves archaeological sites, historical artifacts, and cultural echoes into works that honor courage across time and medium.
Kathleen Caffrey.long: Anthrosercher transforms curiosity into art, exploring archaeological sites, forgotten techniques, and cultural history. Rooted in humanities and classical training, the work spans multiple media to honor those who faced challenges past and present.

Elisha Frontz.short: Elisha Frontz weaves natural patterns and mathematical systems into fiber art.
Elisha Frontz.medium: Elisha Frontz transforms natural patterns and mathematical systems into intricate fiber art pieces that reveal the interconnectedness of all things.
Elisha Frontz.long: Elisha Frontz, a Dayton-based artist with nearly 30 years of experience, creates intricate fiber art inspired by natural systems and mathematics. Her detailed pieces explore the interconnectedness of all things through traditional and fiber mediums.

Rebecca Rowley.short: Rebecca Rowley creates nature-inspired linocut prints rooted in personal meaning.
Rebecca Rowley.medium: Rebecca Rowley crafts linocut prints of wildlife and nature, layering bold ink over watercolor to honor her Ohio woodland roots and deep connections.
Rebecca Rowley.long: Rebecca Rowley transforms grief into art through linocut prints inspired by Monarch butterflies and Ohio's natural world. Working in series of three, she layers black ink over watercolor, creating pieces that forge meaningful connections with viewers.

Heather Caulfield.short: Heather Caulfield captures pure essence and soul through figurative oil paintings.
Heather Caulfield.medium: Heather Caulfield creates soulful figurative paintings, blending traditional techniques with mystical vision. A teacher, author, and spiritual artist.
Heather Caulfield.long: Heather Caulfield is a visionary figurative painter who captures the essence of soul on canvas. Trained by master painters and steeped in Renaissance tradition, she creates spiritually charged works that honor nature and the infinite spirit.

Loura Lawrence.short: Loura captures nature's details with bold colors and photojournalistic flair.
Loura Lawrence.medium: Loura blends photojournalism training with vibrant nature photography, creating bold images that celebrate small subjects and intricate details.
Loura Lawrence.long: Loura transforms everyday details into vibrant visual stories through her photojournalistic lens. Her nature photography features bold colors and realistic subjects, while her handcrafted jewelry at CinnamonAndSilver.com ranges from funky to traditional.

Dayton Ohio Photos (Kevin Myers).short: John Smith captures Dayton's beauty through stunning night and architectural shots.
Dayton Ohio Photos (Kevin Myers).medium: John Smith transforms Dayton Ohio into art through captivating night photography, architecture, and landscapes that showcase the city's hidden beauty.
Dayton Ohio Photos (Kevin Myers).long: John Smith has been photographing Dayton Ohio since 2019, specializing in night photography, architecture, and landscapes. His mission is to showcase Dayton and beyond in its best light, sharing stunning visual stories.

Christine Gaffney.short: Christine Gaffney sculpts with AI and her body, subverting the male gaze.
Christine Gaffney.medium: Christine Gaffney uses AI and her body as sculpture to challenge traditional gazes and explore body politics through interdisciplinary work.
Christine Gaffney.long: Christine Gaffney creates interdisciplinary sculptures using AI-generated imagery and her own body to subvert institutional and gendered gazes. Her work embraces process, incorporating household robots and making construction visible as anti-portraits.

Abiona Venee.short: AV Glass blends hot shop and lampwork techniques in Dayton, Ohio.
Abiona Venee.medium: AV Glass combines hot shop and lampwork expertise, crafting unique glass art in Dayton since 2007 at Front Street Artist Studios.
Abiona Venee.long: AV Glass merges hot shop and lampwork mastery into distinctive creations. Since 2007, dual apprenticeships shaped a unique approach to glass art, now realized in a dedicated Dayton studio at Front Street Artist Studios.

Kate Santucci.short: Kate Huser Santucci explores human interconnection through layered encaustic art.
Kate Santucci.medium: Kate Huser Santucci uses encaustic techniques to explore communication, time, and our evolving relationship with the natural world through layered imagery.
Kate Santucci.long: Kate Huser Santucci creates layered encaustic paintings exploring human interconnection with nature. Her work features microbes, plants, and asemic writing, inviting viewers to discover imagery beneath wax surfaces that reveal our evolving world.

Robert Farley.short: Award-winning photographer capturing landscapes and urban scenes across America.
Robert Farley.medium: Award-winning photographer transforms travels across America into stunning landscape, nature, and urban fine art with over a decade of experience.
Robert Farley.long: This award-winning photographer transforms cross-country travels into stunning fine art photography. Specializing in landscape, nature, and urban scenes, they've captured America's beauty for over a decade with recent honors at Ohio exhibitions.

Terry Welker.short: Terry Welker creates kinetic sculptures that dance with light and air.
Terry Welker.medium: Terry Welker, FAIA, crafts lightweight kinetic sculptures from steel and aluminum that animate space through motion, light, and delicate balance.
Terry Welker.long: Terry Welker, FAIA, is an architect/sculptor who creates suspended kinetic works exploring motion and form. Using steel, anodized aluminum, and crystals, he crafts pieces that engage with natural light and air currents in poetic dialogue.
